pr.title: Fix iref_INC to have a void type
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Corrected the inline helper declaration for iref_INC in src/sysdolphin/baselib/object.h by adding an explicit void return type. The PR body states this was needed because the missing type broke context parsing on decomp.me.
what_changed: Changed the declaration of static inline iref_INC(void* o) to static inline void iref_INC(void* o).
Left the helper body unchanged: it increments HSD_OBJ(o)->ref_count_individual and checks for wraparound/nonzero behavior.
The only code change was one header line, with one line added and one line deleted.
smart_moves: Made the return type explicit instead of relying on invalid or legacy implicit-int style C syntax.
Fixed a header-level declaration issue that affected external tooling, specifically decomp.me context parsing, without altering generated helper logic.
Kept the patch narrowly scoped to the broken inline function signature.
decomp_lessons: Header correctness matters for decomp tooling even when the compiler or local build context may not expose the issue immediately.
Static inline helper functions in shared context headers should always have explicit return types so decomp.me can parse them reliably.
Small type-signature fixes can unblock matching workflows by making exported context parse cleanly.
naming_conventions: iref_INC follows the existing ref_INC-style helper naming pattern for reference-count increment operations.
HSD object access in this header uses the HSD_OBJ(o) macro to reach fields such as ref_count_individual.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic was documented in the PR.
The practical matching/tooling tactic was to keep decomp.me context headers syntactically explicit and parseable by adding the missing void return type.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find other static inline helpers in headers missing explicit return types.
Search object.h and related baselib headers for declarations that may break decomp.me context parsing.
Look for other ref-count helper signatures near ref_INC and iref_INC for consistency.
